mnemonics: BEST FIT: 'Best Estimate Solves The problem For Inconsistent Times' - A mnemonic for remembering that the Least Squares solution provides the best fit for an inconsistent system Ax = b by minimizing the error.
keyPoints: The goal is to find an approximate solution 'x' to the system Ax = b when no exact solution exists.
The solution minimizes the squared Euclidean norm of the residual vector: ||b - Ax||².
The normal equations AᵀAx = Aᵀb provide the formula to calculate the least squares solution.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if the columns of A are linearly independent before using the formula x = (AᵀA)⁻¹Aᵀb. If they are not, AᵀA is not invertible.
Confusing the projection vector p = Ax (which lies in the column space of A) with the solution vector x itself.
